history.content: Completed — proposal execution is fully finished and logged. ### What I changed I applied the approved complexity-based declare_plan gating across all requested surfaces: 1. **`src/gateway/routes/chat.router.ts`** - Updated the `handleChat` system prompt instruction. - Replaced the old **“2+ tool calls => declare_plan”** rule with the new **complexity-based** trigger that excludes quick linear chains. 2. **`src/gateway/tools/defs/agent-team-schedule.ts`** - Updated the **`declare_plan` tool description** to the provided replacement policy text. - Included explicit exclusions for fast multi-tool linear flows so docs and runtime behavior match. 3. **`src/gateway/comms/telegram-channel.ts`** - Appended Telegram guidance sentence: - **“For quick linear Telegram actions, do not declare a plan; reserve declare_plan for complex multi-phase tasks.”** ### Verification - Ran **`npm run build`**. - Build passed (**TypeScript compile success, exit code 0**). ### Outcome - Planning-trigger policy is now consistent across: - interactive runtime prompt behavior, - declare_plan tool definition, - Telegram execution context. - No new files created, no deletions, and no API/tool signature changes.
